title: Repo-map ambient context: code-shaped requests auto-inject a compact codebase map
current_state: repo_map (tree-sitter) exists as a tool but the model must think to call it. For code tasks Pi navigates its own repo by trial-and-error read_file calls — slow and token-hungry.
target_state: When the user message is code-shaped (mentions a .py path, a known module name, or edit verbs ['fix','refactor','implement','bug','test']), a cached compact repo map (~400 tokens: top-level dirs, key modules, public symbols of mentioned files) is appended to the dynamic prompt segment like the prefetch block. Cache invalidates on any modify_file/create_file. Non-code chat pays zero tokens.
migration_plan: 1. Extract repo_map's tree-sitter core into a cached helper (module-level cache + dirty flag set by execution-tool writes).
3. Inject into dynamic_p behind a ModeConfig flag (root only).
4. Tests: code-shaped detection (positive/negative), cache invalidation on write, token cap.
risk_notes: Token cost is the risk — cap hard at ~400 tokens and gate conservatively. RISK_FLAGGED pi_agent.py → interactive.
